Not here to hate, just genuinely disappointed.

After playing Demon’s Souls Remake, I told everyone that my most anticipated game would be whatever Bluepoint worked on next. In my opinion, they were among the best in the business. I still haven’t seen many games this generation that matches the level of craftsmanship they delivered... the visuals, the audio design, the 3D audio implementation, the VFX, and how seamlessly everything was brought together. It felt premium in every sense of the word. So seeing a studio like that get shut down is honestly shocking.

I’ve been a PlayStation supporter for years, and I’m still on PS5, even bought the Pro version. But the direction this generation has taken is worrying. The heavy live service push, the fading meaning of true exclusives, amazing studios being closed, fewer standout single-player titles, and a lack of bold new original IPs from major teams… it doesn’t feel like the PlayStation identity I used to be excited about.

I say this as a fan, not a hater: please rethink this direction. Because if this trend continues into next gen, instead of buying a PS6, I may seriously consider moving to PC like 90% of my friends have done already.
